questions.question: A hiker walks $3$ miles in $4$ hours. If she walks for $6$ hours, how many miles will she cover?
A wildlife reserve has a ratio of $5$ deer to $3$ elk. If there are $15$ deer, how many elk are there?
A vehicle consumes $12$ gallons of fuel to travel $240$ miles. How many gallons will it consume to travel $400$ miles?
A park ranger observes a ratio of $2$ hawks to $7$ eagles. If there are $14$ eagles, how many hawks are there?
A group of friends want to share some trail mix in a ratio of $3:5$. If they have $24$ ounces of trail mix, how many ounces will each person get if there are $4$ friends?
A mountain climber ascends $2,000$ feet in $4$ hours. If she ascends for $6$ hours, how many feet will she climb?
A river flows at a rate of $8$ miles per hour. If a boat travels $32$ miles downstream, how many hours will it take?
A forest has a ratio of $4$ trees to $9$ animals. If there are $36$ animals, how many trees are there?
A camper needs to mix $2$ parts of water to $1$ part of food to make a trail snack. If she uses $6$ cups of water, how many cups of food will she need? Show your work and explain your reasoning.

A national park has a deer population that is increasing at a rate of $5%$ per year. If there are currently $500$ deer, how many deer will there be in $3$ years? Use proportions to solve the problem and show your work.

teacher_rules: Emphasize the importance of labeling and unit conversion in word problems
Encourage students to use proportions to model real-world situations
Remind students to check their units and ensure they are consistent throughout the problem
